hawkish_phrases: a few members saw a case for keeping interest rates at their current levels
domestic inflation remained high and there might be new upward pressure on underlying inflation over the medium term – from both tariffs and fiscal policy
services inflation remained above levels compatible with a sustained return to the inflation target
one member, who upheld a dissenting view
firms' profit margins had been squeezed for some time, which increased the likelihood of cost-push shocks being passed through to prices
dovish_phrases: inflation was currently at around the 2% medium-term target
inflation projected to fall below the target in 2026 before returning to target in 2027
the risk of undershooting the inflation target for a prolonged period
lower energy prices and a stronger euro would continue to put downward pressure on inflation in the near term
most measures of underlying inflation suggested that inflation would settle at around the 2% medium-term target on a sustained basis
summary: The Governing Council cut rates by 25 basis points to 2.0% with almost all members supporting and one dissenting, as inflation stood at target but was projected to undershoot temporarily in 2026 before returning to 2% in 2027.
